title: Witkoff and Kushner Fly to Pakistan for Iran Nuclear Talks; Tehran Denies Direct Meeting Planned
detail: On April 25, 2026, US Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner departed for Islamabad, Pakistan for what the White House characterized as direct talks with Iran. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi had already arrived in Pakistan and met with Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, positioning Islamabad as the formal intermediary channel. Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei publicly denied that any direct US–Iran meeting was planned, stating that Iran's positions would be 'conveyed to Pakistan' — a formulation that preserves the ceasefire while rejecting the US framing of 'direct talks.' The structured diplomatic ambiguity reflects a calculated Iranian posture: a presence in Islamabad that maintains the indefinite ceasefire (extended by Trump on April 21, contingent on Iran producing a 'unified proposal') without conceding to US maximalist demands or establishing the precedent of direct bilateral negotiations under coercive conditions. The core nuclear sticking point remains unresolved: the US demands a 20-year pause on uranium enrichment; Iran has offered 5 years. The first Islamabad round (April 11–12) collapsed after approximately 21 hours over uranium enrichment terms. The April 25 mission — replacing JD Vance's cancelled Pakistan trip — represents a lower-profile but broader diplomatic delegation. Pakistan has staked diplomatic capital on its facilitation role under PM Sharif. Simultaneously, the US naval blockade of Iranian ports and IRGC seizure of vessels in the Strait of Hormuz continue, maintaining the coercive backdrop that both sides have used as leverage. Iranian FM Araghchi's presence in Islamabad — while his ministry publicly denies direct talks — suggests Iran is testing the format for a second round without formally agreeing to one.

media.caption: Iran's FM Araghchi in Islamabad as Witkoff and Kushner arrive; Iran's Foreign Ministry publicly denies direct US–Iran meeting is planned, framing talks through Pakistani intermediaries
US envoys head to Pakistan raising hopes of peace talks with Iran's Araghchi — second round of nuclear diplomacy begins
Iran's FM meets Pakistan officials but rules out direct talks with the US — Euronews live coverage of the April 25 Islamabad talks
